---
title: "Some simple economics of AGI (Catalini et al 2026)"
subtitle: "Notes on selective concepts"
author: "Dan Killian, Posit Assistant"
date: 2-1-2026
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
number-sections: false
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    page-layout: full
execute: 
  keep-md: true
editor: visual
reference-location: margin
---

```{r global_options, include=F, warning=F, message=F, echo=F, error=F}
library(here)
library(knitr)
getwd()
source("../prep (May 2025).r")
```

## Overview

### Core Thesis

### The Central Tension: Two Racing Cost Curves

## The Economic Framework

### Human Time Budget

### Production Function

### Effective Labor Composition

### Cost to Automate vs. Cost to Verify

### The Measurability Gap (∆m)

### The Verifiable Share (s~v~)

### The Four Task Regimes

## Dynamic Engines of Transition

### The Missing Junior Loop

### The Codifier's Curse

### Alignment Drift (τ)

### The Trojan Horse Externality (X~A~)

### Predator-Prey Capital Dynamics

### Parasite vs. Successor: The Dynastic Welfare Function

## Core Model

### Static Measurability Geometry

### Dynamics: Human Experience and Alignment

### Governance Levers

## Strategic Implications

### Measurability-Biased Technical Change

### The Precarious Nature of Human Comparative Advantage

### Bifurcation of Value: Solvable, Verified, and Status Economies

### Hollow Economy vs. Augmented Economy

## Actionable Strategies

### Individuals

### Companies: The AI Sandwich

### Investors

### Policymakers
